purpose: Decomposes the AI-to-productivity lag into distinct phases with quantified historical durations
components: Phase 1: Diffusion — technology availability and initial adoption (shortest phase)
Phase 2: Reorganization — organizational restructuring to exploit technology (60-70% of total delay)
Phase 3: Measurement — statistical infrastructure captures the gains (varies by national statistics capacity)
current_assessment: AI is in late Phase 1 / early Phase 2. The reorganization phase is the binding constraint and has historically taken 60-70% of total lag duration across four GPT analogues.
limitations: Historical calibration from only 4 GPT analogues
Phase 2 duration may be compressible if AI assists its own organizational restructuring (recursive reorganization)
Measurement phase depends on statistical agency capacity and methodology, which varies by country
Phases overlap in practice — the sequential framing is a simplification
